methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Neko Health is a Series B biotech company founded by Daniel Ek and Hjalmar Nilsonne, currently valued at $1.8B post-money with $326M+ raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same stage, alongside 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the typical 1.9x multiplier step-up from Series B to the next financing round, implying a valuation of $3.4B based on the $1.8B last post-money. Peer-median anchoring across four same-sector, same-stage companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median of $805M, with a peer range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target. The median P/S across the three public peers is 5.8x, but cannot be applied without revenue data.

The triangulated analysis across two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $684M to $3.9B, with a median of $2.1B. The current $1.8B valuation sits within this range but closer to the midpoint-to-lower bound. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to peer growth profiles and the dispersion within the peer set, particularly the $2.7B spread between Profluent and Eikon Therapeutics.
